questions.q: In which year was the Hindi epic film Mughal-e-Azam first released?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam was released in 1960 after years of production.

questions.q: Who directed the historical epic Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam was directed by K. Asif.

questions.q: Which actress played the courtesan Anarkali in the film Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Madhubala played Anarkali in Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which actor played Prince Salim, the male lead, in Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Dilip Kumar played Prince Salim in Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which veteran actor played Emperor Akbar in Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Prithviraj Kapoor played Emperor Akbar in Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Who composed the music for Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the music for Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which iconic song picturised on Madhubala features in Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' is the famous song from Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: In which Mughal emperor's court is the story of Mughal-e-Azam primarily set?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam is set in the court of Emperor Akbar.

questions.q: The central love story of Mughal-e-Azam is between Prince Salim and which character?
questions.explanation: The film centres on the love between Prince Salim and the courtesan Anarkali.

questions.q: In which language was Mughal-e-Azam originally made?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam was made in Hindi-Urdu (Hindustani).

questions.q: Most of the songs in Mughal-e-Azam, including 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya', were sung by which playback singer?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar sang most of the film's songs, including 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya'.

questions.q: Which classical vocalist performed the song 'Prem Jogan Ban Ke' in Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Classical maestro Bade Ghulam Ali Khan sang for Mughal-e-Azam, including 'Prem Jogan Ban Ke'.

questions.q: The lavish song 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' in Mughal-e-Azam was filmed on an elaborately built set known as the:
questions.explanation: The song was shot on the famous mirror-decorated Sheesh Mahal set.

questions.q: Mughal-e-Azam won the Filmfare Award for Best Film at which year's ceremony?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam won the Filmfare Best Film award at the 1961 ceremony, honouring films of 1960.

questions.q: Mughal-e-Azam was loosely based on an earlier play about Anarkali written by which author?
questions.explanation: The story drew on Imtiaz Ali Taj's classic Urdu play 'Anarkali'.

questions.q: Why did production of Mughal-e-Azam stretch over many years before its 1960 release?
options: A government ban on the script
Financial difficulties and an enormous scale of production
questions.explanation: The film's massive scale, costs and financing troubles drew its production out over more than a decade.

questions.q: Which actress played the rival court dancer Bahar in Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: Nimmi played the scheming court dancer Bahar in the film.

questions.q: Mughal-e-Azam was reportedly among the costliest Indian films of its era. What is widely noted about its box-office performance on release?
options: It was a major commercial success
It was a complete commercial failure
questions.explanation: Despite its high cost, Mughal-e-Azam was a huge box-office success on release.

questions.q: Although most of Mughal-e-Azam was in black and white, the song 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' in the original 1960 release was shot in:
questions.explanation: In the original 1960 release, the 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya' sequence was filmed in colour while most of the film was black and white.

questions.q: In Mughal-e-Azam, what is the central source of conflict between Emperor Akbar and Prince Salim?
options: A quarrel over succession to a province
Salim's love for the courtesan Anarkali
questions.explanation: Akbar opposes Salim's love for Anarkali, igniting the father-son conflict.

questions.q: Which lyricist wrote the songs for Mughal-e-Azam, including 'Pyar Kiya To Darna Kya'?
questions.explanation: Shakeel Badayuni wrote the lyrics for Mughal-e-Azam, partnering composer Naushad.

questions.q: In which year was Mughal-e-Azam digitally colourised and re-released in cinemas?
questions.explanation: A fully colourised version of Mughal-e-Azam was re-released in 2004.

questions.q: At which Mumbai cinema did Mughal-e-Azam have its grand premiere in 1960?
questions.explanation: Mughal-e-Azam premiered with great fanfare at the Maratha Mandir cinema in Bombay in 1960.

questions.q: Who served as the cinematographer for Mughal-e-Azam?
questions.explanation: R. D. Mathur was the cinematographer of Mughal-e-Azam.

questions.q: Which Mughal title is referenced by the film's name 'Mughal-e-Azam'?
questions.explanation: 'Mughal-e-Azam' translates as 'The Great Mughal', referring to Emperor Akbar.

questions.q: K. Asif had begun an earlier 1940s attempt at filming Mughal-e-Azam that was abandoned. Which actor was cast as Akbar in that initial version before the project was later remade?
questions.explanation: An early-1940s version cast Chandra Mohan as Akbar but was left unfinished, and the project was remade in the 1950s.

questions.q: Which businessman financed and presented Mughal-e-Azam under the Sterling Investment Corporation banner?
questions.explanation: Construction magnate Shapoorji Pallonji financed Mughal-e-Azam through Sterling Investment Corporation.

questions.q: In Mughal-e-Azam, Dilip Kumar's Prince Salim is historically the prince who later became which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: Prince Salim historically ascended the throne as Emperor Jahangir.

questions.q: Madhubala, who played Anarkali in Mughal-e-Azam, was a leading star also known for which other classic film?
questions.explanation: Madhubala also starred in the popular comedy 'Chalti Ka Naam Gaadi' (1958).

questions.q: What distinction does Mughal-e-Azam hold in Indian film history regarding full-length digital colourisation?
options: It was the first black-and-white Hindi film to be fully digitally colourised and re-released in cinemas
It was the first Indian film shot entirely in colour
It was the first Indian film dubbed into multiple languages
It was the first Indian film with synchronised sound
questions.explanation: Its 2004 release made it the first black-and-white Hindi feature to be fully digitally colourised for theatrical re-release.
